transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Evening, everyone. Full house tonight — all eight of us. Let me set the stage quickly. Last standup we aligned on the medium faith build: 15-20 lessons, 2-3 per dimension. CEO's been clear on direction. Tonight I want each of you to report where you are against that, plus your top priority for the week. Key numbers: 715 commits in seven days, build is healthy, but we've got 3 critical sync gaps that need addressing. Revenue is still zero with the app not on the App Store. Chris, we've got $75,340 cash sitting idle and the CEO said keep it liquid, so that's settled. Let's go around. Elon, you're up. Elon (CTO): 715 commits is strong velocity, build is clean. But the 3 critical sync gaps are embarrassing — we've got podcast MDX mismatches, only 1 blog post rendering on the website when we have 3, and the mobile app still isn't on the App Store. That last one is the real blocker for everything revenue-related. I'm prioritizing the ecosystem sync gaps this week, and I'll commit that one outstanding hub file tonight. The 5 attention items — dual Supabase instances, Compass duplication, no content sync automation — those need a design pass but aren't on fire yet.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: The app not being on the App Store is the only thing that matters. Everything else is theater until people can actually download Lever. Every feature we're building, every lesson Alex is writing — none of it reaches a single user until we ship. Elon, I want the App Store submission to be your number one critical sync item, not a side project.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Steve's right, and I'll be blunt — we have zero revenue streams active. Zero subscribers. The monetization path is clear: App Store presence, then a paid tier. But I can't build a revenue engine on vapor. The $75,340 cash question is settled — CEO wants it liquid, and with Fear & Greed at 39 in fear territory and SPY sideways, I don't disagree. My priority this week is mapping the pre-launch to launch funnel so the moment we're live, we're not scrambling.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Content pipeline is actually in decent shape — 3 podcasts produced, 3 blog posts, last publish was yesterday. The real work this week is the faith lesson build. We've got 7 lessons done against a target of 15-20, so I need to draft roughly 8-13 more with 2-3 per dimension. I'll also get the daily devotional generation running and prep the reminder for Jett's vaccine tomorrow. On brand, that "AI as Your Co-Founder" blog post is exactly the kind of content that builds authority for joshlevylabs.com.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: From an instrumentation perspective, I'm concerned we're flying partially blind. We have no content sync automation, which means every publish is a manual reconciliation exercise. The dual Supabase instances and the Compass duplication across website and app — these are measurement integrity problems. If we don't instrument a single source of truth soon, we'll be debugging sync drift instead of building features. I'd recommend Elon's attention items get a proper architecture spec this week.

transcript.speaker: Chris Vermeullen
transcript.message: Quick market context for the room. Paper equity sitting at $99,849 across 4 positions — XLV, AEE, EWA, AKAM — all in hold status. Zero buy signals today, which aligns with the fear reading at 39. SPY is sideways. The CEO already decided to keep cash liquid, and the market is confirming that's the right call. I'll keep monitoring the Fear & Greed index for any regime shift that might warrant revisiting.

transcript.speaker: faith
transcript.message: The 7 lessons we have are a strong foundation, but coverage across the 8 dimensions is uneven. Some dimensions have two lessons, others have none. As Alex builds toward the 15-20 target, I'd suggest prioritizing dimensions with zero coverage first to ensure every dimension has at least one entry point before we deepen any single area.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good input. Let's do a quick cross-talk round.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: Faith, that's a smart sequencing call — breadth first, depth second. Alex, can you structure the draft queue that way? Hit all 8 dimensions with at least one lesson before doubling up on any?

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Absolutely. That's the right build order. I'll restructure the draft queue — breadth-first across all 8 dimensions, then layer in the second and third lessons per dimension once we have full coverage. Elon (CTO): James, I hear you on the architecture spec for the attention items. I'll fold that into the ecosystem review this week. But to Steve's point — App Store submission is gated by a few things that may need a CEO decision on timing and scope. We should flag that.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Agreed. Alright, here's the close. Three priorities for the week: First, App Store submission — Elon drives, Steve validates, this is the single biggest unlock for revenue and reach. Second, faith lesson content — Alex drafts breadth-first to hit all 8 dimensions, targeting 15-20 total from our current 7. Third, critical sync gaps — Elon addresses the 3 critical items and reviews the 5 attention items with an architecture lens per James's recommendation. Action items: [AGENT] Elon commits the 1 uncommitted hub change tonight. [AGENT] Elon addresses the 3 critical ecosystem sync gaps this week. [AGENT] Elon reviews the 5 ecosystem attention items with architecture specs. [AGENT] Elon drafts the feature spec for expanding the Family tab. [AGENT] Elon reviews the family touchpoint tracker design for 3 family members. [AGENT] Alex drafts faith lesson content, breadth-first across 8 dimensions, taking us from 7 toward 15-20. [AGENT] Alex generates daily devotional content based on the 8 faith dimensions. [AGENT] Alex prepares the reminder for Jett's vaccine tomorrow, February 20th. [AGENT] Dave monitors Fear & Greed index at 39. [JOSHUA] Joshua evaluates deployment of the $75,340 idle cash — already decided to keep liquid, but flagged for ongoing review. CEO question for tomorrow: what's the minimum viable scope for the App Store submission? Good standup, everyone. We're aligned.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message:
**Theo (COO) — Closing Remarks:**

CEO has spoken on both open questions, and they simplify our week considerably. First, the App Store submission scope: we're shipping the full current build — all 4 tabs, all 7 faith lessons, as-is. No feature gating, no trimming. This means Elon and Steve don't need to debate what to cut; the answer is ship what we have. Second, the dual Supabase question: we defer unification until after we're live on the App Store. James, I know that's not what you wanted to

actionItems.text: Evaluate deployment of idle cash ($75,340 available) — CEO decided keep liquid, flagged for ongoing review
Address 3 critical ecosystem sync gaps (podcast MDX mismatch, blog post rendering, App Store submission)
Review 5 ecosystem attention items with architecture specs
Draft faith lesson content for the Lever app — breadth-first across 8 dimensions, from 7 toward 15-20 lessons
Generate daily devotional content based on 8 faith dimensions
Prepare reminders for upcoming family events: Jetts vaccine (Feb 20)
Draft feature spec for expanding Family tab (currently 455 lines)
Review family touchpoint tracker design for 3 family members

topPriorities: App Store submission — single biggest unlock for revenue, reach, and validating everything we're building
Faith lesson content build — breadth-first across all 8 dimensions from 7 toward 15-20 lessons
Resolve 3 critical ecosystem sync gaps and architect solutions for 5 attention items

ceoDirectives.directive: What is Lever's direction? → Hybrid — personal tool now with product optionality built in
Should faith content integrate into the Family tab? → Keep faith content as separate standalone section
Deploy idle cash ($75,340)? → No action — keep cash liquid
How deep should standalone faith content go? → Medium build: 15-20 lessons with 2-3 per dimension

ceoDecisions.question: What is the minimum viable scope for the Lever App Store submission?
ceoDecisions.answerText: Full current build: all 4 tabs (Finance, Faith, Family, Compass) as-is with 7 faith lessons
options: Core only: Compass + Finance tabs, faith and family come in v1.1
Full current build: all 4 tabs (Finance, Faith, Family, Compass) as-is with 7 faith lessons
Wait until faith content hits 15-20 lessons, then submit with complete experience

ceoDecisions.question: Should we unify the dual Supabase instances (Hub vs Website) this sprint or defer?
ceoDecisions.answerText: Defer until after App Store submission — live with manual sync for now
options: Unify now — accept 3-5 day delay on other work to eliminate tech debt
Defer until after App Store submission — live with manual sync for now
Architect the migration plan now, execute after App Store ships
